# MTL Token Baseline — Tier-2 dev corpus (`T_tier2-dev`)

<!-- GENERATED by bench/tokcount/report_tier2.py — do not edit by hand. -->

- Date: 2026-07-12
- Corpus version: T_v0.3
- Corpus set: T_tier2-dev (tier2, dev split) — anti-gaming: added AFTER the frozen T_v0 gate, kept in a separate set/file.
- tiktoken version: 0.8.0
- Encodings: o200k_base, cl100k_base (OpenAI tiktoken, public proxy).
- Metric: STATIC program-source tokens. Ratio = tokens(python-idiomatic) / tokens(mtl), same encoding; higher = better for MTL.
- v0.3 solutions use the sequence primitives `(` (Fold, left fold) and `$` (Xor); every v0.3 solution is interpreter-validated in bench/validate/tests/tier2_v03.rs (all I/O vectors Halt).
- Stage: still stage 1 (STATIC program-source tokens). No agent-writability trial yet; that is future work.
- Counting policy: a single trailing newline stripped per file, uniform across variants (identical to report.py / BASELINE.md).
- This file is separate from the frozen bench/BASELINE.md; those T_v0 numbers are untouched (Fold/Xor do not change frozen T_v0).

## Tokenizer availability

- All encoders loaded and encoded successfully.

## Per-task token counts — 13 tier-2 tasks

`WALL` = no solution exists in that MTL tier (inexpressible). single_number was a v0.2 WALL, cleared in v0.3 by `$` (Xor). two_sum / binary_search remain walls in both tiers (indexed access deferred). Ratios compare idiomatic Python to each MTL tier, same encoding.

## Tier-2 aggregate MTL reduction — before / after

Aggregate ratio = sum(idiomatic-python tokens) / sum(mtl tokens) over the named task set, per encoding (same token-sum aggregation as report.py's headline). Three aggregates are reported so the fold effect is isolated from the effect of adding a new cheap task (single_number):

- **v0.2 tier-2** — the 10 v0.2-solvable tasks (single_number excluded: it had no v0.2 solution).
- **v0.3 same-10** — those same 10 tasks re-solved in v0.3 (EXCLUDING single_number), so the delta is purely the fold effect.
- **v0.3 tier-2 (scenario B)** — all 11 v0.3-solvable tasks, INCLUDING single_number (the wall xor clears).

| Aggregate | Tasks | Encoding | idiomatic-py total | mtl total | aggregate ratio |
| --- | ---: | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| v0.2 tier-2 | 10 | o200k_base | 327 | 171 | 1.91x |
| v0.2 tier-2 | 10 | cl100k_base | 324 | 171 | 1.89x |
| v0.3 same-10 | 10 | o200k_base | 327 | 82 | 3.99x |
| v0.3 same-10 | 10 | cl100k_base | 324 | 80 | 4.05x |
| v0.3 tier-2 (scenario B) | 11 | o200k_base | 352 | 91 | 3.87x |
| v0.3 tier-2 (scenario B) | 11 | cl100k_base | 349 | 89 | 3.92x |

**Before / after (o200k):** the v0.2 tier-2 aggregate of 1.91x rises to 3.99x on the same 10 tasks re-solved with Fold, and to 3.87x on the full 11-task scenario B. **cl100k:** 1.89x -> 4.05x (same-10) -> 3.92x (11-task). The sequence primitives roughly DOUBLE the aggregate reduction: the v0.2 accumulator tasks that paid a large stack-juggling tax collapse to short folds.

## Gate verdict — v0.3 tier-2 (scenario B) vs ≥3x

Gate: does the v0.3 tier-2 aggregate over the 11-task scenario-B set clear **≥3.0x**? For contrast, the v0.2 tier-2 aggregate was 1.91x (o200k) / 1.89x (cl100k) — well below the gate.

| Encoding | v0.2 tier-2 | v0.3 tier-2 (scenario B) | gate (≥3.0x) | margin |
| --- | ---: | ---: | :---: | ---: |
| o200k_base | 1.91x | 3.87x | MET | +0.87x |
| cl100k_base | 1.89x | 3.92x | MET | +0.92x |

**Verdict: MET on both encoders.** The v0.3 sequence primitives lift the tier-2 aggregate from ~1.9x (v0.2) to 3.87x (o200k) / 3.92x (cl100k), clearing the 3.0x gate with margin +0.87x / +0.92x. This is a measured, interpreter-validated result — not a projection.

## Expressibility verdict

**11/13 solved in v0.3** · **2/13 inexpressible** (two_sum, binary_search — indexed access deferred). single_number moved wall -> solved via `$` (Xor). Every v0.3 solution is interpreter-validated (bench/validate/tests/tier2_v03.rs, all vectors Halt); the retained v0.2 solutions stay validated in bench/validate/tests/tier2.rs.

| Task | v0.3 verdict | Note |
| --- | :---: | --- |
| sum_list | solved | clean Fold `0[+](` |
| length_list | solved | clean Fold `0[_1+](` |
| product_list | solved | clean Fold `1[*](` |
| max_list | solved | Fold with a keep-larger combinator; was solved-but-ugly in v0.2 |
| min_list | solved | Fold with a keep-smaller combinator; was solved-but-ugly in v0.2 |
| reverse_list | solved | clean cons-Fold `[][~;](`; output is a quotation |
| palindrome_number | solved | unchanged from v0.2 — scalar digit-reverse, no fold/xor applies |
| climbing_stairs | solved | unchanged from v0.2 — scalar iterative DP, no fold/xor applies |
| contains | solved | Fold with a carried target + OR idiom; was solved-but-ugly in v0.2 |
| count_occurrences | solved | Fold with a carried target + running count; was solved-but-ugly in v0.2 |
| single_number | solved | WALL CLEARED: LinRec+Xor `[>0=][0][][$]|`; no bitwise primitive existed in v0.2 |
| two_sum | inexpressible | still a wall — no positional index / enumerate / map |
| binary_search | inexpressible | still a wall — cons-list has no O(1) random access |

## v0.3 measured effect — the fold win (measured, not projected)

The 5 v0.2 accumulator solutions (max/min/reverse/contains/count) that paid the hand-rolled stack-juggling tax collapse under Fold. Combined measured tokens fall from **113 / 113** (v0.2, o200k / cl100k) to **44 / 42** (v0.3) — a **69 / 71 token** reduction realized by the `(` combinator, measured on the actual counted artifacts.

single_number, a v0.2 WALL, is now expressible as the LinRec+Xor solution `[>0=][0][][$]|`, measured at **9 / 9 tokens** (o200k / cl100k) vs its idiomatic Python at 25 / 25 — a 2.78x / 2.78x reduction. (An earlier design note projected a ~5-token bare Fold; the validated LinRec form is 9 tokens. We report the measured number.)

## Caveats

- **Stage 1 only.** This is STATIC program-source token compression. It is necessary but not sufficient; the north-star metric still needs an agent-writability trial (success rate x correction attempts).
- **Interpreter-validated.** Every v0.3 and v0.2 solution is executed on the reference mtl-core interpreter against honest I/O vectors; no hand-trace or projected number is carried into the aggregates.
- **Two walls remain.** two_sum and binary_search are still inexpressible (no positional index / O(1) random access); they carry no MTL denominator and are excluded from every aggregate.
- **Frozen T_v0 untouched.** Fold/Xor do not change the frozen T_v0 corpus; bench/BASELINE.md is unchanged.
